title: Debug a signal handling or shutdown bug
description: Trace which signals are delivered, what the handler does, and where unsafe work or lost signals cause the failure.
body: Steps: 1. Determine which signals are involved (SIGTERM/SIGINT for shutdown, SIGCHLD for child reaping, SIGPIPE for closed sockets) and their disposition. 2. Inspect the handler for non-async-signal-safe calls or long work. 3. Check for lost signals (standard signals do not queue; multiple SIGCHLD can collapse to one, so reap in a loop with waitpid WNOHANG). 4. Verify signals are unblocked where expected and blocked around critical sections. 5. Move real work out of the handler to the main loop. Rollback point: handler changes are reversible. Validation: shutdown completes cleanly under repeated signals and no child becomes a zombie.
